title: Conventionalism
body: Conventionalism is the philosophical attitude that fundamental principles of a certain kind are grounded on (explicit or implicit) agreements in society, rather than on external reality.  Unspoken rules play a key role in the philosophy's structure.  Although this attitude is commonly held with respect to the rules of grammar, its application to the propositions of ethics, law, science, biology, mathematics, and logic is more controversial.
categories: Articles with unsourced statements from January 2008
